sb_prompt: DISCUSS POSSIBLE CHANGES IN THE WAY TRIALS BY JURY ARE CONDUCTED.     FOR EXAMPLE
raw_output:
ParticipantA: Hello!
ParticipantB: Hello! How can I help you today?
ParticipantA: I was just wondering if you have any experience with jury duty.
ParticipantB: Yes, I have been summoned for jury duty a few times. What's on your mind?
ParticipantA: I was just thinking about the way trials by jury are conducted and how they might be improved. Do you have any thoughts on that?
ParticipantB: Well, I've never really thought about it before, but I do think that the way the jury is selected could be improved. It seems like it could be more fair if they had a better way of selecting the jury members.
ParticipantA: I agree. I think that the current system of selecting jurors randomly from a pool of people is not the most effective way of ensuring a fair trial. Do you think there might be a better way to select jurors?
ParticipantB: I'm not sure, but I do think that it would be helpful if there were some kind of screening process to ensure that the jurors are impartial.
ParticipantA: Yes, I think that would be a good idea. It would help to ensure that the jurors are not biased in any way and that they can make an impartial decision.
ParticipantB: Exactly. And I think that it would also be helpful if the jurors were given more information about the case before they have to make a decision.
ParticipantA: I agree. I think that the jurors should be provided with all of the relevant information about the case so that they can make an informed decision.
ParticipantB: Yes, and I think that the jurors should also be given the opportunity to ask questions of the witnesses and the lawyers.
ParticipantA: That's a good idea. I think that would help the jurors to better understand the case and to make a more informed decision.
ParticipantB: Definitely. And I think that the jurors should also be given a deadline to make their decision. That way, the trial would not drag on for too long.
ParticipantA: Yes, I agree. I think that a deadline would help to ensure that the trial is conducted in a timely manner.
ParticipantB: Exactly. Well, I think we've covered a lot of ground here. Do you have any other questions or topics you'd like to discuss?
ParticipantA: No, that's all for now. Thank you for your time and for sharing your thoughts on this topic.
ParticipantB: You're welcome. It was nice talking to you.
